div1.entryFree:
Baseness, 1) low rank: reflect I not on thy b. court-contempt? Wint. IV, 4, 758.
2) that which becomes a low station: some kinds of b. are nobly undergone, Tp. III, 1, 2. such b. had never like executor, Tp. III, 1, 2 I once did hold it a b. to write fair, Hml. V, 2, 34.
3) vileness, meanness: all the accommodations that thou bearest are nursed by b. Meas. III, 1, 15.* Tw. V, 149. Cor. III, 2, 123. Oth. I, 3, 332. III, 4, 27. Ant. IV, 14, 57. Ant. IV, 14, 57 Cymb. I, 1, 142. III, 5, 88.
Abstr. pro concr.: thou unconfinable b. Wiv. II, 2, 21. damned b. Tim. III, 1, 50.
4) illegitimate birth, bastardy: that forced b. which he hath put upon it, Wint. II, 3, 78. why brand they us with base, with b., bastardy? Lr. I, 2, 10.
